EST.10.1.definition: to put (used in a great variety of applications, literal, figurative, inferentially, and elliptically)
achashverosh (i.e., ahasuerus or artaxerxes, but in this case xerxes), the title (rather than name) of a persian king
properly, a burden (as causing to faint), i.e., a tax in the form of forced labor
above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
properly, a habitable spot (as desirable); dry land, a coast, an island
a sea (as breaking in noisy surf) or large body of water; specifically (with the article), the mediterranean sea; sometimes a large river, or an artif

EST.10.2.definition: properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
an action (good or bad); generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product (specifically, a poem) or (generally) property
might or (figuratively) positiveness
force (literally or figuratively); by implication, valor, victory
exposition
greatness; (concretely) mighty acts
mordecai, an israelite
who, which, what, that; also (as an adverb and a conjunction) when, where, how, because, in order that, etc
to be (causatively make) large (in various senses, as in body, mind, estate or honor, also in pride)
a king
not (the simple or abs. negation); by implication, no; often used with other particles
they (only used when emphatic)
to grave, by implication, to write (describe, inscribe, prescribe, subscribe)
above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
properly, writing (the art or a document); by implication, a book
a word; by implication, a matter (as spoken of) or thing; adverbially, a cause
a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an asso
a king
madai, a country of central asia
paras (i.e., persia), an eastern country, including its inhabitants

EST.10.3.definition: (by implication) very widely used as a relative conjunction or adverb (as below); often largely modified by other particles annexed
a jehudite (i.e., judaite or jew), or descendant of jehudah (i.e., judah)
properly, a repetition, i.e., a duplicate (copy of a document), or a double (in amount); by implication, a second (in order, rank, age, quality or loc
achashverosh (i.e., ahasuerus or artaxerxes, but in this case xerxes), the title (rather than name) of a persian king
great (in any sense); hence, older; also insolent
a jehudite (i.e., judaite or jew), or descendant of jehudah (i.e., judah)
to be pleased with; specifically, to satisfy a debt
a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance [like h0001])
properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow (for pursuit or search); by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship
good (as an adjective) in the widest sense; used likewise as a noun, both in the masculine and the feminine, the singular and the plural (good, a good
a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively (of words), to speak; rarely (in a destructive sense) to subdue
safe, i.e., (figuratively) well, happy, friendly; also (abstractly) welfare, i.e., health, prosperity, peace
properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity
